fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Colorado Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity colorado, how to transfer physical therapist license to colorado, physical therapist compact colorado, colorado physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Colorado DORA — Physical Therapy
board.parent_dept: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA), Division of Professions and Occupations
board.mailing_address:
Colorado Office of Physical Therapy
1560 Broadway, Suite 1350
Denver, CO 80202

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 contact hours of continuing competence activities every 2 years

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Colorado.

steps.title: Create Online Account and Start Application
steps.description: Navigate to the DORA Online Services portal (apps.colorado.gov/oss), create a new account, and then select 'Apply for a New License' to begin the Physical Therapist Endorsement application.

steps.title: Complete Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out the entire online application for endorsement and pay the required application fee through the DORA Online Services portal.

steps.title: Complete the Colorado Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Access and complete the open-book Colorado Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically found within the DORA Online Services portal or linked from the application.

steps.title: Submit Verification of Licensure
steps.description: Arrange for official verification of licensure to be sent directly from all other states where you have ever held a physical therapist license (active or inactive) to the Colorado Office of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status via the DORA Online Services portal and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Colorado Office of Physical Therapy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Colorado physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $100. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Colorado have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Colorado participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Colorado without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Colorado?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Colorado
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Colorado by endorsement, you must use the DORA Online Services portal to submit your application and required documentation.

official_links.registration_steps: On the DORA Online Services portal, select 'Register for an Account' to create your login credentials. Once logged in, navigate to 'Apply for a New License' and choose the Physical Therapist Endorsement application.
